hard.next_step: JRA 6/29: Email from Michelle, Director of Procurement. Zycus has been selected as a top two finalist for their S2P transformation project. Next steps could include an additional demo, pricing breakdowns, and implementation discussions. The delay is because they had to make a "netsuite decision" which we offered to help with. Their timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace. Additionally, there is new IT leadership. Jason, project lead is currently facilitating a knowledge transfer of the project before we move forward with next steps.
hard.ais_why:
• This is an active RFP with AI sitting at the center. The 4/20/2026 demo featured AI-powered Merlin Intake for regulated service requests, AI-generated sourcing templates and question libraries (the rep cited roughly 40% faster event build), Effects Scoring Pro using AI to suggest supplier evaluation scores, and Agentic CLM auto-extracting contract metadata. Mark Hannigan was impressed that Merlin Intake configured a complex use case in just two hours.
• More than 20 stakeholders, including Michelle Messier, Daniel Pires, Jason Howard, Chad Berryhill, and Kimberly Roberts, engaged on AI specifics. They probed the supplier-scoring AI logic and got the explanation that it understands the event scope and evaluates every supplier response. Mark also reacted with "Impressive" to multi-ERP integration.
• The RFP is rated at the top tier, Autonomous and Agentic AI (Outcomes), which matches the demo content. With an estimated $500k CRO clinical-trial value, a regulated environment driving the need for AI orchestration, audit-grade two-envelope bidding, and Supplier Risk Radar in play, the full agentic AI value prop landed.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is a confirmed top-two finalist for Roivant/Immunovant's $225k S2P RFP (Michelle Messier email, 29 Jun), a genuinely healthy and engaged deal, but the 29 Jul close is not credible: the buyer paused next steps pending an internal NetSuite decision and Jason Howard's IT-leadership knowledge transfer, and pricing, an ROI case, economic-buyer sign-off, NetSuite integration validation, and contracting are all still outstanding.
north_star_verdict.math: 23 days to close (2026-07-29 from 2026-07-06). In Shortlisted since roughly mid-Mar (created 2026-03-16, qualified 2026-03-17), so ~110 days in early/mid evaluation. Close date already pushed twice: 25 Jun -> 29 Jul on 22 Jun, and 18 Jun -> 25 Jun on 1 Jun. Remaining workstream (finish the NetSuite decision + IT knowledge transfer, additional use-case demo, pricing breakdowns, ROI/EB approval, NetSuite integration validation, MSA/Order Form/SOW) realistically needs 8 to 10 weeks; credible close is Sep 2026.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Upside/Pipeline with close date reset to September 2026; the deal quality supports Upside, the 29 Jul date does not.
evidence: Top-two finalist confirmed by Michelle Messier (Director of Procurement) email, 29 Jun (source: Next_Step__c 2026-06-29)
Buyer paused next steps pending an internal NetSuite architecture decision and IT-leadership knowledge transfer led by Jason Howard (source: Next_Step__c 2026-06-29)
Buyer explicitly listed pricing breakdowns, an additional demo, and implementation discussions as the next steps, none of which have happened (source: Next_Step__c 2026-06-29)
No pricing, ROI, or commercial discussion on any call or activity in the 120-day window (source: Salesforce activities)
Coupa replace is a confirmed forcing function keeping the timeline tight (source: Next_Step__c 2026-06-29)
Amount cut from $300k to $225k on 12 May by Michael McCarthy (source: OpportunityFieldHistory)
Last Salesforce activity 2026-07-01; last substantive buyer call was the 20 Apr demo (the 8 May and 18 May sessions had the notetaker removed, so no recorded content)

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well established but not yet quantified into a deal-specific value case. On the 20 Apr demo the clinical-services use case was built around an estimated $500k CRO engagement ('it will be 500k as an input', Ronit walking the Merlin Intake flow, 20 Apr), and AIS_Why__c cites an estimated $500k CRO clinical-trial spend as the value anchor. The demo also claimed a ~40% reduction in sourcing-event build time. However, no ROI, savings target, cycle-time or headcount impact has been co-built WITH the buyer, and no commercial conversation has started. For a $225k spend the economic buyer will need a defensible number tied to the CRO spend, so converting the $500k baseline into a quantified savings case is the metrics unlock.

economic_buyer.narrative: No budget owner is confirmed or engaged. Michelle Messier (Director of Procurement) owns and communicated the top-two selection (Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), but Director-level procurement rarely holds final sign-off on a $225k enterprise SaaS purchase. Ian Rosenblum (Chief Information Officer) sits on the Contact Roles and the deal is now gated by an IT-owned NetSuite decision plus new IT leadership in transition, so IT clearly influences the outcome, yet there is no evidence Ian controls the commercial budget. The likely budget authority is Finance (Jess Marenco, Associate Director, Accounting Operations, is a mapped finance path) or an as-yet-unmapped CFO. Mapping and engaging the true budget owner through Regina is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Ian Rosenblum CIO; Jess Marenco, Accounting Operations)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and observed on the 20 Apr demo, which the buyer ran to a provided script across two use cases (clinical CRO sourcing-to-pay and contract expiry/renewal management). Buyer probes revealed the real criteria: agentic AI automation (Mark Hannigan asked how long Merlin Intake took to configure, praising the two-hour answer), native NetSuite integration ('as part of this project, Zycus will connect with NetSuite'), regulated-industry controls (two-envelope bidding, SOX-aligned audit logs, PHI/HIPAA supplier tiering with InfoSec questionnaires), user-access governance (Regina Oliver: business users must not be able to launch RFQ/RFP/RFI), and resilient approval routing (Kimberly Roberts asked what happens when a PO owner is terminated mid-approval). These are confirmed criteria, not inferred.
sources: Avoma demo 2026-04-20 (Mark Hannigan, Regina Oliver, Kimberly Roberts, Daniel Pires, Michelle Messier)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, script-driven RFP: a 24 Mar scoping/Q&A call, script-led S2P demo 20 Apr, a follow-up demo 18 May and a Q&A 8 May (both with the notetaker removed), then a top-two down-select communicated by Michelle Messier on 29 Jun. The process is currently paused on two buyer-internal dependencies: an internal NetSuite architecture decision and an IT-leadership knowledge transfer run by Jason Howard (project lead), which must resolve before pricing, the additional demo, and implementation discussions resume. The formal approval chain (who signs off after procurement) is not yet documented and is the main process unknown.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not begun. This is a new-business full S2P suite deal (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake), so a close will require an MSA, Order Form, and a separately signed SOW, plus InfoSec/compliance review given the regulated pharma and PHI/HIPAA context surfaced on the 20 Apr demo. None of legal, procurement channel, MSA/SOW, or InfoSec has been discussed on any call or activity. Visibility will come from starting the implementation and commercial discussions Michelle Messier requested on 29 Jun, which is where paper and InfoSec scoping surface.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and owned by Procurement with IT as co-owner. Roivant/Immunovant is replacing Coupa (the incumbent, 'their timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace', Next_Step__c 2026-06-29) and needs to manage high-value, regulated CRO clinical-trial spend (~$500k use case) with less manual back-and-forth, audit-grade compliance (SOX, PHI/HIPAA), contract-hierarchy visibility (Michelle Messier asked whether pulling a supplier surfaces the master plus all SOWs and amendments, 20 Apr), and NetSuite as the system of record. The pain owners are Regina Oliver / Michelle Messier in Procurement and Ian Rosenblum's IT organization for the NetSuite/integration dimension.

champion.narrative: Two developing champions, neither yet a strong internal seller. Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations, Contact Role: Evaluator) led the 20 Apr demo, articulated the CRO spend pain, and drove the use-case script, evidence of an active evaluator. Michelle Messier (Director of Procurement) owns the vendor-selection communication and personally delivered the top-two finalist news (29 Jun) and the 29 May thank-you, signalling process ownership and a positive lean. What has developed: both are advancing Zycus through the RFP and pulling the next steps. What is still missing: neither has introduced Zycus to the economic buyer, shared the other finalist's identity or the Coupa-exit reasons, or co-authored an internal ROI case. Converting Regina into an ROI co-author and securing an EB introduction is the champion-building move.

competition.narrative: Zycus is one of two finalists (Michelle Messier, 29 Jun); the other finalist is not yet named. Coupa is the incumbent being displaced, and the 'Coupa replace' is a live forcing function on the timeline (Next_Step__c 2026-06-29). GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are the broader RFP field per Competitors__c but show no active buyer preference. Zycus's wedge is native NetSuite integration (positioned out-of-box, 1,100 APIs) plus agentic Merlin AI and regulated-pharma controls (two-envelope bidding, SOX audit trails, supplier risk radar) that landed on the 20 Apr demo. Mike McCarthy (VP Sales, ex-Coupa) is an asset to preempt an incumbent win-back. The gap is that the other finalist's identity and the buyer's Coupa-exit reasons are unknown, limiting finalist-specific positioning.
sources: Avoma demo 2026-04-20 (NetSuite/AI wedge; Mike McCarthy ex-Coupa)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum was buyer-pulled through the 29 Jun top-two selection but is now paused buyer-side pending an internal NetSuite decision and Jason Howard's IT knowledge transfer; the close date has been pushed twice and the amount trimmed once.

GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are named in the RFP field but show no current buyer preference and rank below the active finalist.

champion_strength.summary: Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations) is the primary champion: she led the 20 Apr demo and articulated the CRO spend pain, and Michelle Messier drives the selection communications. Both advance Zycus, but neither has introduced the economic buyer, shared the other finalist's identity, or co-authored an internal ROI case, so the advocacy is developing rather than strong.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic AI story landed strongly: Merlin Intake, AI sourcing templates (~40% faster build), Effects Scoring Pro, and Agentic CLM were demonstrated live, with Mark Hannigan reacting 'Impressive' to a two-hour configuration. AIS is 9.0 Strong and consistent with the calls.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 9.0 / Status Strong, corroborated by the 20 Apr demo where buyer stakeholders probed the AI supplier-scoring logic and praised Merlin Intake configuration speed; the field is accurate, not over-rated.

reasons.detail: Amount cut 25% from $300k to $225k on 2026-05-12 by Michael McCarthy while the full S2P product scope was retained, a defensive commercial trim that may signal cost pressure or a phased implementation.

Roivant Sciences ($225k S2P, forecast 'Upside Key Deal', stage Shortlisted) had its close date pushed from Jun 25 to Jul 29 on 2026-06-22 by owner Justin Ajmo. The buyer is stalled on an internal NetSuite architecture decision and IT knowledge transfer that must finish before pricing, an additional demo, and implementation scoping can proceed, so the new date rides entirely on their internal timeline. The deal is genuinely live (Zycus named top-two finalist on 6/29, replacing incumbent Coupa on a tight timeline), but with no Economic Buyer yet identified the forecast date can slip again and this quarter-key deal could slide out of the quarter.
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Justin why the forecasted Roivant close slipped to Jul 29, whether the buyer's NetSuite decision has a firm date, and who the Economic Buyer (CFO/VP Finance) is now that we are a top-two finalist.
reasons.summary: Forecasted $225k Roivant deal slipped close date 34 days to Jul 29
reasons.evidence: recent_movements_14d: CloseDate old '2026-06-25' new '2026-07-29' by Justin Ajmo (at 2026-06-22); next_step 6/29 'delay is because they had to make a netsuite decision ... timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace'

commentary.win_position: Win 48 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut 25% from $300k to $225k on 2026-05-12 by Michael McCarthy while the full S2P product scope was….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 30 is anchored on the stage win (48), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Roivant Sciences Ltd — Shortlisted · $225K · closes 29 Jul
cro_panel.intro: Zycus is a confirmed top-two finalist for Roivant/Immunovant's $225k S2P RFP with AI differentiation that landed (Merlin Intake impressed Mark Hannigan on 20 Apr), but the buyer paused on 29 Jun pending an internal NetSuite decision and an IT-leadership knowledge transfer, and with no pricing, no ROI case, and no budget owner engaged, the 29 Jul close will slip.
